newsletter.subject: waiting for the first fireflies to emerge
newsletter.title: before the warm nights arrive
newsletter.opening: it's 11:47pm and miso is pressed against the window, watching rain streak the glass in slow rivulets. the may air carries that particular coldness that makes you pull your sleeves over your hands, even though part of you knows the warm evenings are coming. in a few weeks, the first fireflies will blink awake in the tall grass behind the house, and everything will feel possible again.
newsletter.section1_body:
there's something about this in-between time that makes me think about anticipation as its own kind of presence. not the restless kind that wants to rush toward what's coming, but the quiet kind that knows how to hold space for becoming. the cold rain against warm windows creates a threshold — we're still in the territory of sweaters and tea, but we can almost taste the evening air that will soon carry the scent of honeysuckle and the soft percussion of night sounds.

i've been thinking about how we tend to live in the future tense when seasons shift, always reaching toward what we imagine is coming rather than noticing what's actually here. but tonight there's something beautiful about the not-yet-ness of it all. the fireflies are still gathering their light somewhere beneath the soil and bark, and the warm nights exist only in cellular memory and hope.

maybe there's wisdom in this waiting that we miss when we're eager for the next thing to arrive. the way anticipation can become a form of presence if we let it. sitting here with miso in the lamp light, watching rain blur the world outside, i can feel both the chill of tonight and the warmth of june evenings that haven't happened yet — both real, both asking to be noticed.
newsletter.quote: anticipation as its own kind of presence, not rushing but holding space
newsletter.section2_body:
juni brought over pressed flowers last week — violets and early clover she'd been saving between the pages of an old notebook. we spent the evening arranging them in small glass frames, working mostly in silence except for the occasional 'look at this one' or 'the light caught it perfectly here.' there's something about working with things that have already completed their season that feels like a conversation with time.

it reminded me that making doesn't always mean creating something from nothing. sometimes it means noticing what already exists and finding a way to honor it, to give it a second life in a new form. the pressed flowers will carry a bit of april into august, a small bridge between what was and what will be.
newsletter.prompt: what season are you anticipating right now, and what does the waiting feel like in your body?
newsletter.closing: i hope you're finding moments of gentle anticipation wherever you are tonight. the kind that doesn't rush but simply acknowledges that good things take their own time to emerge. whether you're waiting for fireflies or something else entirely, it's okay to be exactly where you are in the becoming.
newsletter.ps: the first firefly of the season always surprises me, even when i'm looking for it
newsletter.note_wednesday: spent the afternoon watching rain collect in the garden. miso has appointed himself chief puddle supervisor. there's something about these slow may days that makes everything feel suspended in the most beautiful way.
newsletter.note_friday: juni and i are planning to set up blankets in the backyard as soon as the fireflies emerge. some rituals are worth the wait.

youtube_description.title: music for introverts waiting for summer fireflies 🌟
youtube_description.body: hey friend, here's a soft space for those cold may evenings when you're dreaming of fireflies and porch light conversations. sometimes we need music that holds both where we are and where we're going, that honors the beautiful ache of anticipation. this one's for sitting by windows and imagining the warm grass under bare feet.
youtube_description.song_line: ✨ drift into summer dreams to chasing fireflies: https://youtu.be/7SXAG51wZmY
